app.dsa-solver.page.givenAnArrayOfIntege:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
app.dsa-solver.page.givenTheHeadOfASingl: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
app.dsa-solver.page.givenAStringSContain:
Given a string `s` containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

An input string is valid if open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets and closed in the correct order.
app.dsa-solver.page.compilingAndRunningA: Compiling and running against test cases...
app.dsa-solver.page.runYourCodeToSeeTheT: Run your code to see the test results here.
app.interview-prep.page.whatIsAClosureIn: What is a closure in JavaScript?
app.interview-prep.page.aClosureIsTheCom: A closure is the combination of a function bundled together (enclosed) with references to its surrounding state (the lexical environment). In other words, a closure gives an inner function access to the outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned.
app.interview-prep.page.explainTheDiffer: Explain the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases.
app.interview-prep.page.sqlDatabasesAreR: SQL databases are relational, table-based, have predefined schema, and scale vertically (buying bigger servers). NoSQL databases are non-relational, document/key-value/graph-based, have dynamic schemas, and scale horizontally (adding more servers).
app.interview-prep.page.howDoYouDetectAC: How do you detect a cycle in a linked list?
app.interview-prep.page.youCanDetectACyc: You can detect a cycle using Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (also known as the Tortoise and the Hare algorithm). You use two pointers: a slow pointer that moves 1 step at a time, and a fast pointer that moves 2 steps at a time. If there is a cycle, the fast pointer will eventually meet the slow pointer. If they hit null/end, there is no cycle.
app.interview-prep.page.howDoYouHandleCo: How do you handle conflict with a co-worker or teammate?
app.interview-prep.page.usingTheStarMeth: Using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result): I stay calm, listen actively to their perspective without interrupting, focus on the objective/problem rather than personal feelings, identify a compromise or shared goal, and check in later to ensure a positive working relationship. For example, when a teammate and I disagreed on code architecture, we listed the pros and cons of both, consulted senior guidelines, and chose the one that met performance requirements better.
app.interview-prep.page.whatAreReactHook: What are React hooks, and why were they introduced?
app.interview-prep.page.hooksAreFunction: Hooks are functions that let you 'hook into' React state and lifecycle features from function components. They were introduced in React 16.8 to resolve class component issues: reusing stateful logic without changing component hierarchy, avoiding complex lifecycle methods, and keeping related logic grouped together.

app.page.theNextgenDevbhoomiProgram: The Nextgen Devbhoomi program was the turning point of my college life. The instructors provided deep practical insights that are not taught in college classes. The DSA solver helped me crack product interviews.
app.page.comingFromATier3CollegeIn: Coming from a tier-3 college in Uttarakhand, I lacked confidence and resources. Through the Devbhoomi scholarship program, I learned React, web architecture, and got mentored directly by industry experts. I landed my dream job!
app.page.theLibraryAndCodingBootcam: The library and coding bootcamps are fully community-oriented. Working on open-source projects under a personal mentor gave me hands-on confidence. Highly recommend this foundation to all developers.

app.page.yesNextgenDevbhoomiFoundati: Yes, Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is a registered NGO under the Societies Registration Act and holds 80G tax-exemption approval. All contributions qualify for a 50% tax deduction under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act.
app.page.whoIsEligibleToApplyForT: Who is eligible to apply for the IT Internship Program?
app.page.theProgramIsOpenToUndergr: The program is open to undergraduate and postgraduate technical students (B.Tech, BCA, MCA, BSc IT) as well as self-taught developers. We give special placement priority to students belonging to regional communities in the Himalayan foothills.
app.page.howDoesTheDigitalLibraryS: How does the Digital Library support student learning?
app.page.theDigitalLibraryProvidesF: The Digital Library provides free access to curated engineering books, curriculum guides, interview reference sheets, and standard test prep materials. It is designed to save students from purchasing expensive academic resources.

app.faqs.page.title: Frequently Asked Questions
app.faqs.page.subtitle: Got questions? We've got answers. Explore the categories below to learn more about our programs, courses, volunteer opportunities, and donations.
app.faqs.answers.general1: Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is a registered non-profit organization dedicated to bridging the digital divide by providing technical education, career mentorship, and resources to students and aspiring professionals, particularly in the Himalayan region and tier-3 locations.
app.faqs.answers.general2: Our main coordinating office is located Near Shiv Mandir, Dew, Aurangabad, Bihar - 824125, India, with outreach programs extending across the region.
app.faqs.questions.programs1: Who can enroll in your courses?
app.faqs.answers.programs1: Our courses are open to students from technical backgrounds (B.Tech, BCA, MCA, BSc IT, etc.) as well as self-taught coding enthusiasts. We prioritize candidates from underserved communities and tier-3 colleges.
app.faqs.questions.programs2: Are your courses free of charge?
app.faqs.answers.programs2: Yes! Most of our core training bootcamps, library resources, and mentorship programs are sponsored through CSR partnerships and individual donations, making them free of cost for selected students.
app.faqs.questions.programs3: Do you provide job placement support?
app.faqs.answers.programs3: Yes. We work closely with recruiting partners to provide mock interviews, resume feedback, portfolio reviews, and direct placement opportunities to students who successfully graduate from our advanced bootcamps.
app.faqs.questions.donations1: Is my donation eligible for tax exemption?
app.faqs.answers.donations1: Yes, Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is a registered NGO under Indian law. All donations qualify for tax exemption under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act.
app.faqs.answers.donations2: 100% of individual donations directly sponsor student scholarship stipends, setting up rural computer labs (infrastructure), and procuring learning materials. Administrative and core operational expenses are funded separately.
app.faqs.answers.volunteering1: Yes, we have standard remote volunteer roles for developers, designers, and industry professionals. You can mentor students online, conduct mock interviews, or contribute to our open-source tools.
app.faqs.questions.volunteering2: How do I apply as a volunteer?
app.faqs.answers.volunteering2: You can go to our Volunteer page and submit an application highlighting your background and areas of interest. Our team will contact you for a brief onboarding session.
app.privacy.page.title: Privacy Policy
app.privacy.page.subtitle: Your privacy matters to us. Learn how Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation collects, uses, protects, and manages your personal information.
app.privacy.sections.intro.title: 1. Introduction
app.privacy.sections.intro.content: Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation ("we", "us", or "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and share personal information of users who visit devbhoomifoundation.org, enroll in our courses, make donations, or apply as volunteers.
app.privacy.sections.collect.title: 2. Information We Collect
app.privacy.sections.collect.content: We collect information you provide directly to us when using our platform or communicating with us. This includes: name, email address, phone number, academic details, resume link, payment details for donations, and communication preferences. We also collect automated usage data like IP addresses and browsing behavior via cookies to improve user experience.
app.privacy.sections.use.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
app.privacy.sections.use.content: Your information is used to: provide and maintain our educational services, manage student registrations, process donations and issue tax-deductible receipts, coordinate volunteer activities, send regular project updates, and ensure security on our website.
app.privacy.sections.sharing.title: 4. Data Sharing & Security
app.privacy.sections.sharing.content: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information. We may share data with trusted third-party service providers (like secure payment gateways) who assist us in operating our platform. We employ standard technical and administrative safeguards (like SSL encryption) to protect your personal data from unauthorized access or alteration.
app.privacy.sections.cookies.title: 5. Cookies & Tracking
app.privacy.sections.cookies.content: We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to monitor activities on our website and hold certain user sessions. Cookies help us analyze web traffic, remember preferences, and offer a more personalized experience. You can manage cookie preferences in your browser settings.
app.privacy.sections.rights.title: 6. Your Privacy Rights
app.privacy.sections.rights.content: You have the right to request access to the personal data we hold about you, request corrections to inaccurate data, or ask for the deletion of your data when it is no longer required for legitimate service delivery. To exercise these rights, please contact us.
app.privacy.sections.thirdparty.title: 7. Third-Party Links
app.privacy.sections.thirdparty.content: Our website contains links to external platforms, such as payment gateways or social media sites. We do not control and are not responsible for the privacy practices of these third-party websites. We encourage you to review their privacy policies before providing personal info.
app.privacy.sections.changes.title: 8. Changes to this Policy
app.privacy.sections.changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Effective Date." Your continued use of our services after updates constitute acceptance of the revised policy.
app.privacy.sections.contact.title: 9. Contact Us
app.privacy.sections.contact.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at nextgendevbhoomi@gmail.com or at our office Near Shiv Mandir, Dew, Aurangabad, Bihar - 824125, India.
app.terms.page.title: Terms & Conditions
app.terms.page.subtitle: Please read these Terms & Conditions carefully before using our platform or participating in our programs.
app.terms.sections.agree.title: 1. Agreement to Terms
app.terms.sections.agree.content: By accessing or using devbhoomifoundation.org ("Website") or enrolling in programs offered by Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation ("Foundation"), you agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you must not use this Website.
app.terms.sections.property.title: 2. Intellectual Property Rights
app.terms.sections.property.content: Unless otherwise stated, all materials on the Website, including course curricula, software code, design templates, graphics, and articles, are the intellectual property of Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation. You may access these resources for your personal, non-commercial educational use only. Unauthorized replication, sale, or distribution is strictly prohibited.
app.terms.sections.accounts.title: 3. User Accounts & Security
app.terms.sections.accounts.content: To access certain services or courses, you may need to register an account. You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information and maintain the security of your login credentials. You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account.
app.terms.sections.conduct.title: 4. Student & Member Code of Conduct
app.terms.sections.conduct.content: Students, volunteers, and mentors are expected to maintain a professional, respectful learning environment. Harassment, plagiarism, discrimination, sharing offensive material, or using the platform for commercial advertising is strictly prohibited and can result in immediate termination of access.
app.terms.sections.donations.title: 5. Donations & Payments
app.terms.sections.donations.content: All financial transactions (donations, sponsorships) are executed securely. By donating, you certify that the funds are your own. The issuance of tax-exempt receipts under Section 80G is automated and sent via email. Detailed refund terms for payments are governed by our Refund Policy.
app.terms.sections.liability.title: 6. Limitation of Liability
app.terms.sections.liability.content: Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation provides its services on an "as is" and "as available" basis. We make no warranties that services will be uninterrupted or error-free. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Foundation shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages resulting from the use or inability to use our services.
app.terms.sections.indemnity.title: 7. Indemnification
app.terms.sections.indemnity.content: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation, its trustees, employees, volunteers, and partners from and against any claims, losses, liabilities, and expenses arising out of your violation of these Terms & Conditions.
app.terms.sections.governing.title: 8. Governing Law & Jurisdiction
app.terms.sections.governing.content: These Terms & Conditions shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising under these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in Aurangabad, Bihar, India.
app.terms.sections.modifications.title: 9. Modifications to Services
app.terms.sections.modifications.content: We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any course, program, or feature on our platform at any time without prior notice. We shall not be liable to you or any third party for any such modifications or service suspensions.
app.terms.sections.contact.title: 10. Legal Contact Information
app.terms.sections.contact.content: For any legal inquiries or clarifications regarding these Terms & Conditions, please contact us at nextgendevbhoomi@gmail.com.
app.refund.page.title: Refund Policy
app.refund.page.subtitle: Learn about our donation refund practices, program cancellation processes, and timeline guidelines.
app.refund.page.statCourseDesc: 100% refund for withdrawal requests within a week
app.refund.page.statTimelineDesc: Credited back to original source payment mode
app.refund.sections.intro.title: 1. Overview & Purpose
app.refund.sections.intro.content: Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation is committed to transparency. This Refund Policy outlines the terms under which contributions, program sponsorships, and course fees (where applicable) are managed. As a registered NGO, we handle funds with maximum accountability.
app.refund.sections.donations.title: 2. Donation Refunds
app.refund.sections.donations.content: Because donations are immediately allocated to active projects and 80G tax receipts are generated, all voluntary donations made to Nextgen Devbhoomi Foundation are non-refundable. Exception: In case of verified double-debits, duplicate payments, or technical transaction errors, refund requests submitted within 7 days of payment will be honored and processed.
app.refund.sections.courses.title: 3. Paid Courses & Bootcamps
app.refund.sections.courses.content: For specialized premium tracks that involve registration fees, a full 100% refund is available if cancellation is requested prior to the official commencement date. A prorated refund is available for withdrawal requests submitted within the first 7 calendar days of the course starting. No refunds will be provided after the 7-day period has elapsed.
app.refund.sections.events.title: 4. Event Ticket Cancellations
app.refund.sections.events.content: For paid tickets to our workshops, networking sessions, or hackathons, a full refund will be issued if the cancellation request is submitted at least 48 hours prior to the scheduled start time of the event. Requests made within 48 hours of the event are non-refundable.
app.refund.sections.request.title: 5. How to Request a Refund
app.refund.sections.request.content: To request a refund, please send an email to nextgendevbhoomi@gmail.com. You must include the following details in your email: Full Name, Registered Email, Contact Number, Transaction ID, Payment Date, and the reason for requesting a refund. Our support team will review and reply within 48 hours.
app.refund.sections.processing.title: 6. Processing & Payout Timeline
app.refund.sections.processing.content: Approved refunds will be processed back to the original source payment method (UPI, Debit/Credit Card, or Net Banking) within 5 to 7 business days from the date of approval. We are not responsible for any delay caused by banking systems or intermediary processors.
